commands.repair_reason: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the PR is protected-labeled, draft, conflict-marked, and requires public CLI/product acceptance after the stale branch is refreshed.; Needs attention: The entries command reuses the guarded loader, but the current PR head exposes stale ClawHub/npm fallback metadata that current main intentionally hardened.; Review finding: [P1] Preserve ClawHub-only install metadata — src/plugi... (sha=5bcb4a40bb8da988c34a0a59142d3a7cb40eb72d)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` with feed selection, offline snapshot mode, JSON/text output, docs, and focused CLI/catalog tests.

PR surface: Source +1445, Tests +1687, Docs +14, Generated +26, Other +5. Total +3177 across 20 files.

Reproducibility: not applicable. this is a feature PR rather than a current-main bug report. Current main has marketplace refresh/list commands but no entries command.
commands.review_followup: - [P2] Manual review is needed because the PR is protected-labeled, draft, conflict-marked, and requires public CLI/product acceptance after the stale branch is refreshed.

target.title: Add marketplace feed entries command
target.body:
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:start -->
## Hosted Marketplace Feed Stack

This is PR8 in the hosted marketplace feed stack. The stack should land in order, with maintainer review at each step before the later PRs wire hosted marketplace data into broader user flows.

1. #95846: refactor the bundled external plugin catalog toward the feed-shaped data model.
2. #95868: add the lazy hosted marketplace feed loader for the configured public feed URL, with guarded HTTPS fetch, checksum, schema, size, timeout, and bundled fallback behavior.
3. #95877: add accepted hosted snapshot fallback so a failed or unchanged hosted fetch can reuse the last valid feed payload.
4. #95964: persist hosted marketplace feed snapshots in OpenClaw state.
5. #95969: add marketplace source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.
6. #95981: add configurable `marketplaces.feeds` and `marketplaces.sources` profiles.
7. #96155: add `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` as the explicit manual snapshot refresh path.
8. #96158: add `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` as the explicit manual read path over hosted, snapshot, or bundled fallback entries.
9. #96194: add marketplace feed telemetry.

The broader follow-up after this stack is to decide whether and how marketplace entries should feed install/search/startup behavior, add automatic conditional refresh using `ETag` / `Last-Modified`, or move to ClawHub producer-side validation for `/v1/feeds/plugins` and `/v1/feeds/skills`.
<!-- hosted-feed-stack:end -->

## Summary

This is PR8 in the hosted OpenClaw marketplace feed stack. It adds the first explicit read path over the configured hosted marketplace feed: `openclaw plugins marketplace entries`.

The command lists normalized entries from the configured/default OpenClaw marketplace feed and reports whether the data came from a fresh hosted payload, an accepted hosted snapshot, or bundled fallback data. It also supports `--offline` so maintainers can prove the refreshed snapshot path without fetching the live feed.

## Stack Context

- RFC: openclaw/rfcs#19 defines the hosted marketplace feed direction.
- PR1: shape the bundled external plugin catalog as feed-like data.
- PR2: lazy hosted feed fetch from the configured public marketplace feed.
- PR3: hosted snapshot fallback.
- PR4: persistent snapshot storage in OpenClaw state.
- PR5: source profile validation for hosted feed install candidates.
- PR6: configurable marketplace feed/source profiles.
- PR7: `openclaw plugins marketplace refresh` manual snapshot refresh.
- PR8: this PR, `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` manual feed/snapshot listing.

## What Changed

- Adds `openclaw plugins marketplace entries` with `--feed-profile`, `--feed-url`, `--offline`, and `--json`.
- Adds an explicit `offline` mode to the hosted catalog loader so callers can read the latest accepted snapshot without doing a network request.
- Normalizes listed entries into id, label, kind, package/version, and install metadata.
- Updates the plugin CLI docs to include `marketplace entries` and `marketplace refresh`.
- Adds focused CLI and hosted catalog tests for parser dispatch, JSON output, bundled fallback output, and offline snapshot reuse.

## Validation

- `pnpm exec oxfmt docs/cli/plugins.md src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxlint --deny-warnings src/cli/plugins-cli.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.runtime.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `CI=1 timeout 240s node scripts/test-projects.mjs src/cli/plugins-cli.lazy.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-refresh.test.ts src/cli/plugins-cli.marketplace-entries.test.ts src/plugins/official-external-plugin-catalog.test.ts`
- `timeout 300s pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `codex review -c model=gpt-5.5 --base origin/feeds-hosted-catalog-marketplace-refresh`

## Review Note

The final Codex review reported no discrete correctness issues. Its internal sandbox could not run tests because the read-only review sandbox could not create the heavy-check lock directory, but the local targeted tests above passed.

## Real Behavior Proof

Behavior or issue addressed:
`openclaw plugins marketplace entries --offline` reads the persisted hosted marketplace snapshot and lists normalized entries without making a fresh hosted request.

Real environment tested:
Windows checkout `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\pr3` at pushed PR8 head `5bcb4a40bb`, source CLI runtime harness via `node --import tsx`, same state dir previously refreshed by PR7.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

1. Ran PR7 refresh against `https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins` in `C:\tmp\openclaw-feeds-proof\state-pr7-refresh`.
2. Switched to PR8 head.
3. Set the same runtime marketplace config snapshot.
4. Ran `runPluginMarketplaceEntriesCommand({ feedProfile: "clawhub", offline: true, json: true })`.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "source": "hosted-snapshot",
  "entryCount": 59,
  "metadata": { "url": "https://clawhub.ai/v1/feeds/plugins", "status": 200, "checksum": "sha256:90a122c19017ef2c7a0607c8c1cb893ae7871537fe649d39080a52547bed0a7f" },
  "feed": { "id": "clawhub-official", "generatedAt": "2026-06-25T01:19:39.629Z", "sequence": 11 },
  "snapshot": { "savedAt": "2026-06-26T05:30:53.509Z" },
  "error": "hosted catalog feed offline mode",
  "firstEntry": { "label": "Expedia Travel", "id": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw", "version": "1.0.4", "install": { "clawhubSpec": "clawhub:@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw@1.0.4", "npmSpec": "@expediagroup/expedia-openclaw@1.0.4" } }
}
```

Observed result after fix:
The entries command returned `source: hosted-snapshot`, listed 59 entries from the persisted snapshot, preserved feed metadata, and normalized install specs for display/read paths.

What was not tested:
This proof intentionally uses offline snapshot mode, not a fresh hosted request. Fresh hosted refresh is covered by PR7.
target.author: giodl73-repo

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
